rulings.Case Title: BERNARD GREGORY VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, line 2. Defendant The Pep Boys - Manny Moe & Jack of California's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate the plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to products purchased at a Pep Boys store. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims because even if the declaration of Mr. Cirelli is admissible in its entirety, when read in conjunction with Mr. Paxton's deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue whether decedent purchased auto parts from a Pep Boys store. Plaintiffs' objections to Joseph Cirelli's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, line 3. Defendant Champion Industrial Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue Three (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to Issues One and Two (negligence and products liability). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issue Three, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Raymond Townsend was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to address decedent's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Decedent's deposition testimony would be and is admissible because decedent is unavailable, defendant was a party to decedent's personal injury action and defendant had the right and opportunity to cross-examine decedent with an interest and motive similar to that which it has in this action. (Evid. Code § 1291.) However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, decedent's deposition testimony and the declaration of Charles Ay would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. In that case, defendant's objections to decedent's deposition testimony and to Mr. Ay's declaration would be overruled. Summary adjudication of Issues One and Two is taken off calendar as plaintiffs have dismissed their negligence and products liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HOLMES, MICHELLE, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO AND et al VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, line 4. Defendant CalPortland Company's motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained.
